message: Hello! I'm your BPMN Modeling Agent. I'll analyze the task now and start planning the diagram.
I've analyzed the task and defined 6 goals for the model.
Iteration 1 — setting up all pools and lanes (participants structure) before modeling the process flow.
Modeling the full vacation-request process across HR Portal, Direct Supervisor, HR Department (incl. escalation to HR Manager), and Employee notifications.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 10 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 9 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 1/3…
Fixing the collaboration errors by reconnecting all message flows so collapsed pools (Supervisor, Employee) are endpoints themselves (no internal elements).
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 5 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 1 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 2/3…
Fixing the critical layout issue by moving the collapsed Employee pool so it no longer overlaps the collapsed Supervisor pool (message flows remain connected to the pool IDs).
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 2 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 2 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 3/3…

Fixing the remaining critical layout issue: PoolEmployee overlaps the expanded PoolHRDepartment. I will move PoolEmployee down so no participant rectangles intersect; message flows stay connected to the PoolEmployee pool endpoint.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 4 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ After 3 correction attempt(s), 2 critical issue(s) remain. Please review manually.
Remove the content from Direct Supervisor. It's a blackbox. Also the content from Employee. Fix the Start Event from Portal.
I'll update the collaboration so "Direct Supervisor" and "Employee" are true blackboxes (no internal flow nodes) and I’ll fix the "HR Portal" start event usage.

I will (1) keep both "Direct Supervisor" and "Employee" as true blackbox (collapsed) pools with no internal elements (already satisfied in this model), and (2) fix the HR Portal start so it is message-based (triggered by Employee submission) instead of a none start.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 6 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 1 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 1/3…

Fixing the critical BPMN error: PoolHRDepartment is expanded but currently has two StartEvents. I will keep a single StartEvent and route to either 'Open case' or 'Review escalation' via a gateway triggered by incoming messages.

Fixing the critical BPMN error in PoolHRDepartment by ensuring it has exactly one StartEvent and routing both incoming triggers (case creation vs escalation) behind that single start via an event-based wait + gateway. This resolves the expanded-pool '2 StartEvents' violation without changing Supervisor/Employee blackbox pools.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 6 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 3 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 2/3…
I’ll fix PoolHRDepartment so it has exactly one StartEvent, remove the pseudo-start behavior of CatchEscalationReceived, and restructure GwHRDeptTrigger to a proper event-based split without cycles.

I’ll fix PoolHRDepartment so it has exactly one StartEvent, remove the pseudo-start behavior of CatchEscalationReceived, and rebuild GwHRDeptTrigger as a proper event-based split (no cycles) that waits for either 'case created' or 'escalation received'.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 6 issue(s) — details below.
⚠️ 4 critical issue(s) detected. Automatically starting correction pass 3/3…

I’ll fix the HR Department trigger pattern to have exactly one StartEvent, make both incoming triggers reachable after it via the existing EventBasedGateway, and remove the duplicate 'case created' message flow into HR Department. This also eliminates the invalid pseudo-start usage of CatchEscalation.
I've reviewed the completed model. Found 6 issue(s) — details below.
